Who I Am

About Me

I'm a Computer Science student at Chapman University in Orange, CA, specializing in game development and computer graphics. My work lives at the crossroads of algorithmic thinking and interactive design — I care deeply about how systems are built and how they feel to use.

As the Coordinator and Original Developer of Endless Board, my capstone project, I led the design and implementation of a full chess-based rogue-like game built in Unity — handling everything from game logic and card systems to UI architecture and team coordination.

Relevant coursework includes Unreal Engine (GAME 246), Computer Graphics (CPSC 360), and Data Structures & Algorithms (CPSC 350). When I'm not coding, I'm into graphic design, hiking, and music.

Capstone Project

Endless Board

Chess · Card Game · Rogue-like

Endless Board

Devise strategies using chess pieces and cards with unique abilities to reclaim the board from enemies.

Screenshots

IEEE Game Competition — Finalist

IEEE Competition — Cal State Fullerton

Endless Board was selected as a finalist in the IEEE student game competition held at Cal State Fullerton, competing against teams from neighboring colleges across Southern California.

The team showcased the game live at the event, demoing it to judges and attendees — complete with a full chess board setup and custom game art on display.

Endless Board is a turn-based chess rogue-like where players build a deck of cards representing chess-piece troops and tactical spells, then deploy them on a chessboard to defeat waves of enemies and reclaim the board.

Each run is unique — you recruit new troops like the IronMarch Queen, Metal Mage, and The Wall, collect power cards that apply the Fortify mechanic, and make strategic decisions across a branching map of Battle, Duplicate, Purge, Recruit, and Shop nodes.

As Coordinator and Original Developer, I designed and built the core game loop, card system, Fortify mechanic, battle logic, map generation, and shop system — all while managing a small development team through Chapman University's capstone program.
